name: Flax (Neural Networks on JAX)

performance.notes: JAX backend; XLA compilation; TPU-native; faster than PyTorch at scale on TPUs

agent_readiness.notes: Neural network library built on JAX by Google. NNX (new API): mutable module system similar to PyTorch nn.Module. Linen (legacy API): functional module system. Handles parameter management in JAX's functional paradigm. Used by HuggingFace (Flax model implementations), Google Brain, and top ML research. T5X, MaxText (Google's LLM training framework) use Flax/JAX. Partner library with Optax (optimizers) and Orbax (checkpointing).

gotchas: NNX (new) vs Linen (old) API migration ongoing
Smaller ecosystem than PyTorch (fewer pretrained models and tutorials)
Not suitable for most production deployment pipelines
best_when: Training large models on TPUs (Google's recommended stack)
When HuggingFace Flax model implementations are needed
Large-scale distributed training on TPU pods
avoid_when: Most practical applications (PyTorch ecosystem more practical)
summary: Flax is Google's neural network library built on JAX. Provides module system (NNX/Linen) for building neural networks with JAX's functional transforms. Used by HuggingFace for Flax model implementations and Google for large-scale TPU training. Best for research requiring JAX's composable transforms or TPU-native training. PyTorch remains more practical for most applied ML.
